stem: A loan application enters the loan processing pipeline. The probability of passing application review is 83%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing credit check is between 71% and 85% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing underwriting is 80%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing approval is 98%. What is the overall probability that the loan application is approved?

ground_truth.answer: P(approved) ranges from 0.462 to 0.553 as the credit check pass rate varies from 71% to 85%. Point estimate: 0.508.

solution_steps: The loan application must pass all 4 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3)×P(stage 4).
The uncertainty in the credit check stage creates a 9.1 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the credit check rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
